_description: Curated panel of clinically actionable focal CN events. Two complementary lists: (a) AMPLIFICATIONS — well-validated cancer-driver oncogenes commonly amplified to high copy number (HER2/ERBB2, MYC, MDM2, CDK4, etc.), with each entry annotating typical lineage / clinical relevance; (b) EXTENDED_DELETIONS — tumor suppressors NOT already in DepMap's inferred-LoF panel (BAP1, SMARCA4, ARID1A, STK11, BRCA1/2, etc.). The existing 8 in DepMap inferred-LoF (RB1, TP53, PTEN, NF1, CDKN2A, VHL, MTAP, APC) are intentionally excluded here to avoid duplication — those continue to be surfaced via the inferred.functionalLoss field. Edit this file and re-run scripts/process_clinical_cn.py to extend.

thresholds.rationale: Amplification: ≥ 3.0 (≥ 6 actual copies in a diploid baseline). Strong amplification: ≥ 5.0 (focal high-level). Deletion: ≤ 0.5 (single-copy loss). Deep deletion: ≤ 0.3 (near-homozygous, biologically equivalent to LoF).

amplifications.context: HER2-amplified breast / gastric. Trastuzumab / T-DM1 / T-DXd indication.
Pan-cancer driver. Burkitt lymphoma (IGH-MYC), neuroblastoma, breast, colon, etc.
Defining event for high-risk neuroblastoma. Also retinoblastoma, medulloblastoma.
Small-cell lung cancer subset.
GBM, NSCLC. EGFR amplification often co-occurs with EGFRvIII variant in glioma.
Breast, gastric — HER3 amplification rare but relevant for HER3-targeting agents.
Liposarcoma (defining), osteosarcoma, glioma. MDM2 inhibitor indication in TP53-WT context.
Pan-cancer; alternative p53 inhibitor — co-amplification with MDM2 sometimes.
Liposarcoma (often co-amped with MDM2), glioma, melanoma. CDK4/6 inhibitor indication.
T-ALL, glioma, breast. Same drug class as CDK4 amp.
MCL (CCND1-IGH), breast, HNSCC. Cyclin-D1 overexpression — CDK4/6 inhibitor candidate.
Testicular germ-cell tumors, others.
Burkitt lymphoma, multiple myeloma.
HGSOC, gastric. Cyclin-E1 amp confers CHK1/WEE1 inhibitor sensitivity.
Lung squamous, breast. FGFR inhibitor candidate.
Gastric, endometrial, ICC (intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma).
Bladder (often with FGFR3 mutation), MM (FGFR3 fusion).
Gastric, NSCLC (often co-occurs with MET exon-14 skipping). Tepotinib / capmatinib / crizotinib.
GIST (often hotspot mut + amp), melanoma.
Glioma, GIST, IMT.
Pan-cancer. Amplification distinct from KRAS hotspot mutations — less common but biologically distinct.
Castration-resistant prostate cancer (key resistance mechanism). Androgen receptor amp.
Breast — ER amp / mutation in endocrine-therapy-resistant lines.
Melanoma — amplification in ~15% of metastatic melanoma; lineage survival oncogene.
Lung squamous, esophageal, HNSCC — lineage-survival amp.
Lung adeno (TTF-1 amp); lineage-survival oncogene.
Follicular lymphoma (BCL2-IGH); also amplification in DLBCL. Venetoclax indication.
HGSOC, MCL, breast. Aurora kinase A inhibitor candidate.
Glioma, others.
Rare amp; mainly fusion-driven (TRK inhibitors).
NUT carcinoma — fusion-driven, but amp can co-occur.
Mostly promoter-mutation driven, but amplification reported in glioma.
Various cancers; less common amp event but actionable with IGF1R inhibitors.

extended_deletions.context: Mesothelioma, uveal melanoma, RCC, cholangiocarcinoma. Defining BAP1-deficient tumors.
Small cell carcinoma of the ovary hypercalcemic type (SCCOHT — defining), lung, others. SWI/SNF complex.
Rhabdoid tumors (defining), epithelioid sarcoma. SWI/SNF complex.
Ovarian clear cell, endometrial, gastric. Most-deleted SWI/SNF subunit pan-cancer.
Lung adenocarcinoma (often co-mutated with KRAS, immune-cold phenotype). Same as LKB1.
HR-deficient breast / ovarian. PARP inhibitor synthetic lethality.
CLL, mantle cell lymphoma, prostate. PARP / ATR inhibitor sensitivity.
Often co-deleted with CDKN2A but tracked separately.
Lung adenocarcinoma — most events are mutational but deletions occur.
Pan-cancer — mostly mutational but deletions in colorectal / T-ALL.
Myeloid neoplasms — mostly mutational, deletion rare.
